Human data support mild stress reduction and liver enzyme normalization in hepatitis populations.

name: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum)
summary: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) is a yellow-flowered herb with well-studied antidepressant effects for mild-to-moderate depression. Its active constituents (hypericin, hyperforin) inhibit reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy comparable to low-dose SSRIs for mild depression, with fewer side effects in that population.

name: Turkey Tail
summary: Turkey Tail (Trametes versicolor) is a shelf mushroom yielding two extensively studied polysaccharopeptides: PSK (krestin) and PSP. These compounds stimulate NK cells, macrophages, and T-cell activity, and PSK is approved in Japan as an adjunct cancer immunotherapy. Human evidence for standalone immune support in healthy populations is limited but supportive.

name: Turmeric (Curcuma longa)
summary: Botanical profile with evidence, safety, and practical fit.

name: Valerian
summary: Valerian evidence is mixed: older systematic review/meta-analysis found possible subjective sleep/anxiety benefit and no severe adverse events, while umbrella-review evidence remains weak/inconclusive for insomnia. Extract quality and whole-root/rhizome preparation matter.

name: Valeriana officinalis
summary: Valerian may support sleep onset and nighttime relaxation, with results varying by root preparation.

name: Yohimbe
summary: Yohimbe is represented here as a bark-derived monograph anchored to yohimbine, an alpha-2 adrenergic antagonist with sympathomimetic effects.
